description: Machine-readable constraint registry distilled from SDD/ (IBM TeamSD) and AGENTS.md for the mrrc_ft710 codebase. Consumed by sdd_context.py (context | check | hook). severity: block = reject the edit, warn = allow but surface, info = guidance shown in context output only.

rules.title: DN; is step-DOWN, never send it
rules.message: On the FT-710, 'DN;' is NOT a DNR query — it steps the active VFO DOWN ~20 Hz per call. Polling it caused a live frequency-drift incident (V1.2). DNR level is intentionally not polled.

rules.title: Compressor PR mapping is 0=OFF/1=ON
rules.message: Use PR00=OFF / PR01=ON. The Yaesu PDF (1=OFF/2=ON) is a known errata — sending PR02 kills TX audio.

rules.title: Tuner AC mapping is AC000/AC001/AC003
rules.message: This codebase deliberately maps the ATU to AC000 (off) / AC001 (on) / AC003 (tune start), not the Hamlib AC010/AC011 forms. See SDD gap-analysis §1.1 before changing.

rules.title: SH set format is SH00NN
rules.message: Filter width SET is SH00NN (SH + P1=0 + P2=0 + 2-digit index), e.g. f"SH00{idx:02d}". The SH0NN form is silently ignored by the radio. Set handlers must also read SH0 back (~150 ms) and broadcast the actual index.

rules.title: Serial I/O only inside CatController
rules.message: All CAT serial I/O goes through CatController (asyncio.Lock serialization, asyncio.to_thread offload, 20 ms post-write pacing). Do not touch a raw serial object from other modules.

rules.title: No hardcoded secrets
rules.message: Secrets come from environment variables (FT710_WEB_PASSWORD etc.), never literals. Do not commit passwords, serial-device paths, or local driver assumptions.

rules.title: No inline JS logic in index.html
rules.message: index.html is the SPA shell only. UI logic goes into ft710_ui.js or static/modules/ (vanilla JS, camelCase). Bump the ?v= cache-buster on edited assets.

rules.title: Audio rates are 44.1k device / 48k codec — never 16k
rules.message: 16 kHz capture was the V1.0/1.1 'crackling' bug. Codec domain is 48 kHz (Opus mandates it), FT-710 USB device domain is 44.1 kHz; audio_resample.py is the only SRC bridge (960↔882 = 20 ms).

rules.title: PyAudio streams open at 44100
rules.message: PyAudio device streams run at 44100 (FT-710 USB native rate). 48000 belongs to the Opus/codec domain only; resample at the server boundary via audio_resample.py.

rules.title: Mutate state via RadioState.update()
rules.message: Assigning state fields directly bypasses dirty-field tracking, so clients never see the change. Use radio.update(field=value) (deliberate bulk re-sync via setattr in the watchdog is the one exception).

rules.title: WebSocket endpoints require token auth
rules.message: Any new WebSocket endpoint must validate the ?token= query param against _auth_tokens before accept, like the existing four. Tokens are cleared on server restart.

rules.title: Deployment values via env vars
rules.message: Serial ports and other deployment-specific values belong in environment variables (FT710_SERIAL_PORT, FT710_BAUD_RATE, ...), not source literals.

rules.title: TX0 is fire-and-forget — no verify loops
rules.message: The 3×200 ms post-release TX0 verify loop was removed in V1.2 (cost ~600 ms per release; the radio obeys the first write). Stuck-keyup detection = 500 ms TX-status poll + browser PTT watchdog + dead-man switch. Do not re-add a blocking verify.

rules.title: Poll loops discard stale in-flight reads
rules.message: After every awaited poll query, re-check _should_skip(field) and _polling_paused() before applying the value — a response in flight when the user's set arrived is stale (V1.7 SSB filter race). Set handlers must call skip_next_poll BEFORE the CAT write.

rules.title: Latency-critical CAT uses the priority path
rules.message: PTT/TUNE-class commands go through send_priority_set_command() (_cancel_polls cooperative abort). Poll loops and _read_until must stay cancel-aware; keep poll query timeouts at 0.25 s to bound lock occupancy.

rules.title: Documentation sync is part of the change
rules.message: A change that alters behavior, commands, state fields, or conventions also updates: affected SDD chapters, SDD/14-version-history.md (new entry), SDD/README Quick Facts version, AGENTS.md module table/conventions, README.md, tests/README.md counts.

rules.title: Tests: hardware-free, unittest, counts synced
rules.message: Tests are stdlib unittest, named test_*.py, and must run without radio/serial/audio hardware (mock at the boundary). Add a regression test for every bug fix. Keep tests/README.md module/test counts accurate.

context_map.notes: All server logic lives in server.py (AD-001) with modular sibling imports; lifespan via @asynccontextmanager. Set handlers: call scheduler.skip_next_poll() BEFORE the CAT write, then radio.update() so the change broadcasts. PTT/tune use the priority path.

pyserial sync API + asyncio.to_thread; all I/O serialized through _lock; 20 ms post-write pacing; set commands are write-only (fire-and-forget); queries read with prefix matching. CAT errata: DN forbidden, PR 0/1, AC 000/001/003, SH00NN.

7 cooperative tasks (IF 100 ms / VFO 500 ms / TX status 500 ms / TX meters / settings 2 s / slow 5 s / watchdog 1 s). Re-check skip AFTER each query await (stale-read guard). Query timeout 0.25 s. Never poll DN;.
Dataclass + _dirty_fields; to_dirty_dict broadcasts only changes; derived properties (mode_name, band_name, filter_hz, s_unit...) live in the DERIVED map — new derived fields register there.

Two rate domains: codec 48 kHz (Opus, browser) vs device 44.1 kHz (FT-710 USB). Exactly one SRC step per direction at the server boundary (audio_resample.py, 960↔882). Frames carry a 1-byte codec tag (0x00 PCM / 0x01 Opus). RX silence watchdog feeds rx_audio_silent.
FT4222 SPI runs in the scope_pipe subprocess (ctypes hangs must not stall asyncio); identical binary frame format for real FFT and S-meter Gaussian fallback; server degrades gracefully on pipe exit.

Single source of truth for mode/band/filter tables, S-meter + meter calibration, poll intervals, constants. UI-facing tables must stay consistent with static/ counterparts (there is a consistency test).

Vanilla JS, camelCase; UI rendering in ft710_ui.js or static/modules/ — never inline in index.html. Client merges partial stateUpdate into local radioState mirror. Bump ?v= cache-busters on edited assets (service worker bypasses JS/HTML but version strings guard the rest).
unittest discover -s tests; no hardware; every regression gets a test; sync tests/README.md counts when adding modules/tests.

Doc edits that reflect runtime changes get a 14-version-history.md entry and a SDD/README.md Quick Facts version bump. SDD describes the runtime as-is — if the SDD is wrong, fix the SDD in the same commit as the code.
